what_it_does: Implements a local SQLite ledger for wizard-run-1: it defines a schema for files, links, blocks, proposed interpretive marks, and run provenance, plus helper functions to connect, initialize, upsert records, and log runs. It also includes an inline self-test that exercises the schema end-to-end.
observations: Uses SQLite with WAL mode and JSON1 support (json_extract in selftest).
Schema stores design-substrate metadata: principle, framing, reach, surface_claims, form_specific, substance.
Files are upserted by relative path with JSON-encoded frontmatter, surface_claims, and form-specific data.
Marks table captures proposed annotations with confidence, source pass/tier, evidence JSON, and a 'proposed' status.
Runs table records run_id, kind, model, timestamp, and params for provenance.
Self-test deletes the existing database, initializes it, writes sample records, and asserts expected output.
standouts: Schema explicitly models interpretive/design substrate concepts (principle, framing, reach, surface_claims, substance).
Marks table is structured like an evidence/annotation ledger with confidence and source attribution.
Inline self-test demonstrates and validates the full data path in one file.
conventions: Uses sqlite3 directly with os.path.expanduser for a home-directory database path.
Serializes dicts/lists to JSON via a private _j helper.
Uses INSERT ... ON CONFLICT for idempotent upserts and INSERT OR IGNORE for links.
Sets PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL for concurrent-read safety.
concerns: Self-test unconditionally removes the database at DB if it exists, which is destructive.
Database path is hardcoded in the user's home directory with no configuration hook.
No schema versioning or migration mechanism.
No functions to transition marks from 'proposed' to accepted/rejected or to resolve conflicts.
No foreign-key constraints or referential cleanup between files, links, blocks, and marks.
notes: A small, self-contained SQLite substrate module that captures file metadata, relationships, interpretive marks, and run provenance for a design/code ledger.
questions: What downstream process consumes or resolves the proposed marks?
What do the 'substance', 'principle', 'framing', and 'reach' fields mean semantically?
What is the expected run_id format and how are runs chained or compared?
Is this module intended to stay single-writer forever, or will concurrency be added later?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is an idempotent, provenance-stamped SQLite substrate for a wizard run.
intention_signals: It appears designed to create a durable, queryable record of a design/code corpus so that multiple passes can add evidence-backed annotations and trace every change back to a run.
novelty: It merges a code/design substrate schema with an evidence/confidence mark table and run-level provenance in a single local SQLite module, validated by an inline self-test.
contribution: Provides a concrete SQLite schema and CRUD helpers for persisting file metadata, links, content blocks, proposed interpretive marks, and run provenance in an idempotent, single-writer style.
summary_for_embedding: A SQLite-backed ledger module defining tables and helpers for files, links, blocks, proposed interpretive marks, and run provenance, with an inline self-test.
intention_for_embedding: To give wizard-run-1 a durable, queryable substrate for capturing design/code artifacts and evidence-stamped interpretive marks.

symbols.does: Opens a SQLite connection with WAL mode enabled.
symbols.description: Returns a sqlite3 connection to the hardcoded DB path and sets PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL to support concurrent reads during single-writer operation.

symbols.does: Creates the ledger schema and indexes if they do not exist.
symbols.description: Uses conn() and executescript(SCHEMA) to set up tables for files, links, blocks, marks, runs, and supporting indexes, then commits and closes.

symbols.does: Serializes a value to a JSON string with non-ASCII characters preserved.
symbols.description: A small helper used throughout the module to store dicts and lists as JSON text in the SQLite tables.

symbols.does: Inserts or replaces a file record with extracted substrate metadata.
symbols.description: Writes a row to the files table including form, JSON frontmatter, principle, framing, reach, surface_claims, form_specific, substance, run_id, and timestamp; on conflict it updates all fields.

symbols.does: Records a directed src-to-dst relationship, ignoring duplicates.
symbols.description: Inserts a link row with INSERT OR IGNORE so the same (src, dst) pair can be added idempotently.

symbols.does: Appends a typed content block associated with a file.
symbols.description: Inserts a row into the blocks table linking a relative file path to a block kind and raw content string.

symbols.does: Creates a proposed interpretive mark with confidence and evidence.
symbols.description: Inserts a mark row with type, value, confidence, source pass/tier, JSON evidence, default 'proposed' status, and timestamp.

symbols.does: Records metadata about an analysis or capture run.
symbols.description: Inserts or replaces a run row keyed by run_id with kind, model, timestamp, and JSON params, providing provenance for other records.
